ks1.objective: Catch the ball as it comes up the line, then throw it at the stumps to score.
successCriteria: Hands ready in front of the body to catch
Calls for the ball before it arrives
Throws at the stumps (any accurate technique)

lks2.objective: Practise catching under pressure and hitting stumps to simulate getting a batter out running.
successCriteria: Soft hands on the catch — gives with the ball
Picks the right throw type for the distance (underarm close, overarm far)

uks2.objective: Combine quick fielding, accurate throwing and team rotation under a time limit.
successCriteria: Decisive choice of underarm vs overarm based on distance
Hits stumps in 2 or fewer attempts at distance

space.description: Groups of 5. A wicket-keeper stands behind the stumps at the front. 5 cones are laid out in a line going AWAY from the stumps, with one player at each cone. The player at cone 5 (furthest back) starts with the ball.

steps: Set up: groups of 5, with one player at each of 5 cones in a line. Stumps at the front. Wicket-keeper behind the stumps.
The player at cone 5 (back) starts with the ball and throws to the player at cone 4.
Cone 4 catches, throws to cone 3. Continue up the line.
When the ball reaches the FRONT player, they catch, run to the cone immediately in front of them, and throw the ball at the stumps.
Rotation: the thrower at the front follows their ball and becomes the new wicket-keeper. The old WK collects the ball and goes to the back of the queue. The rest of the team moves up one cone.

errors.issue: Throwing at stumps from behind the cone
errors.fix: No-score that throw; reset the player

errors.issue: Wrong throw type for the distance
errors.fix: Pause and ask: 'how far? what throw?'
Number the cones; call rotation out loud

progressions: Make it easier: decrease the distance between cones; decrease the distance to the stumps.
Make it harder: increase distance between cones / stumps. Add a time limit (e.g. score 3 in 90 seconds).
questions: How did you know which type of throw to use?
What's the trade-off between underarm and overarm?
What helps you make an accurate throw under time pressure?

step.space: Adjust cone-to-cone and cone-to-stumps distances to tier
step.task: Specify underarm-only or overarm-only for technique focus
step.equipment: Soft / larger balls for KS1; standard cricket balls for UKS2
step.people: Groups of 4 or 6 also work — add/remove cones to match

base.showmanship: Encourage following up — the team that recovers the ball fastest restarts soonest. Praise good rotation and quick decisions on throw type. Call out specific accuracy: "Great underarm at close range, Sam."
